title: OpenCode plugin: per-session WS peers (GH #93)
description:
Implements GitHub issue prassanna-ravishankar/repowire#93. Today the OpenCode plugin opens one WebSocket per opencode server and routes everything through a single 'primary' root session, so any other root sessions in the same opencode instance are invisible to the mesh. Blocks the workflow of running multiple sessions inside one opencode and addressing each from telegram/dashboard/peers.

Approach: each root session gets its own peer_id and WS connection. Subagent (non-root) sessions stay attributed to their parent root.

Plugin file: repowire/installers/opencode.py (PLUGIN_CONTENT).

Full design and acceptance criteria captured in GH #93 comment: https://github.com/prassanna-ravishankar/repowire/issues/93#issuecomment-4412217552
design:
Source-grounded facts (verified against opencode repo via peers):
- session.created/updated/deleted on plugin event bus; root = !info.parentID (packages/opencode/src/session/session.ts:308-327).
- All session/message events carry sessionID — disambiguation reliable.
- Plugin tool execute(args, ctx) receives ctx.sessionID (packages/plugin/src/tool.ts:4-21,32-36; packages/opencode/src/tool/registry.ts:137-158; packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:424-433).
- info.title suitable for display; cache key projectPath#sessionId.
- No per-session prompt mutex in opencode — plugin must guard against two concurrent promptAsync at same sessionID.
- Daemon WS transport is 1 peer per connection — N WS per opencode server is the model.

Plan: replace module singletons with peerBySession Map<sessionId, PeerConn>; on init enumerate root sessions; route all events by sessionID; tool callbacks use ctx.sessionID for from_peer attribution; bump cache filename for migration; busy guard on promptAsync; system.transform injects per-session peer identity; permission.ask attributes by sessionID; notifications prefixed '@from → targetPeer:' since tui.prompt.append has no per-session target.

value: opencode plugin tool.execute(args, ctx) receives ToolContext with sessionID/messageID/agent/directory/worktree (packages/plugin/src/tool.ts:4-21 in opencode repo). Plugged through at packages/opencode/src/tool/registry.ts:137-158 (fromPlugin) and constructed in packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:424-433. Means repowire opencode plugin can attribute tool calls to the originating session for per-session peer routing (issue #93).
